currentPosition.description:
As Director of Engineering, I lead three strategic teams that drive innovation, operational excellence, and AI adoption across the organization:

B2C Team: Oversee the continued development and scaling of TailorMed’s first B2C product, ensuring seamless performance, user experience, and technological growth.

Data Operations Team: Responsible for the company’s core data, maintaining its integrity, accuracy, and reliability. The team ensures that all data processes and pipelines run efficiently to support analytics, decision making, and operational excellence.

AI Development Team: Lead the adoption of AI driven engineering, transforming manual and repetitive processes into intelligent, automated workflows that boost efficiency and innovation.

Beyond internal leadership, I also manage integrations with customers and strategic partners, leading technical discussions, architectural planning, and cross company collaboration to ensure smooth interoperability and shared success.

In this role, I combine strategic vision, technical expertise, and cross-functional leadership to guide teams in building scalable, data driven, and AI-enhanced solutions that strengthen TailorMed’s impact in the healthcare technology ecosystem.

experience.description:
As Director of Engineering, I lead three strategic teams that drive innovation, operational excellence, and AI adoption across the organization:

B2C Team: Oversee the continued development and scaling of TailorMed’s first B2C product, ensuring seamless performance, user experience, and technological growth.

Data Operations Team: Responsible for the company’s core data, maintaining its integrity, accuracy, and reliability. The team ensures that all data processes and pipelines run efficiently to support analytics, decision making, and operational excellence.

AI Development Team: Lead the adoption of AI driven engineering, transforming manual and repetitive processes into intelligent, automated workflows that boost efficiency and innovation.

Beyond internal leadership, I also manage integrations with customers and strategic partners, leading technical discussions, architectural planning, and cross company collaboration to ensure smooth interoperability and shared success.

In this role, I combine strategic vision, technical expertise, and cross-functional leadership to guide teams in building scalable, data driven, and AI-enhanced solutions that strengthen TailorMed’s impact in the healthcare technology ecosystem.

As a Software Engineer Manager, I led two key teams driving major innovation and integration initiatives within the company.

I guided the B2C development team, continuing the evolution of the company’s first ever B2C product overseeing architecture, implementation, and scaling strategies to ensure high quality delivery and long term sustainability.

In parallel, I established and led a new integration team responsible for connecting TailorMed’s core platform with the system of a recently acquired company. This work focused heavily on data engineering, including large scale data migration, transformation, and synchronization between both systems. I ensured a smooth transfer of millions of records, seamless integration with TailorMed components, and stable operation under big data conditions.

This role allowed me to combine technical leadership, cross team collaboration, and architectural decision making to deliver scalable, reliable, and business critical solutions.

experience.description:
In the SCA team, we scan our customers’ code repositories to identify open source packages, vulnerabilities, and licenses, helping them mitigate these issues efficiently. By integrating our findings with other data sources like IaC, SAST, and production environment data, we aim to create a world-class Application Security Posture Management (ASPM) product.

We serve some of the most significant companies globally, managing operations at a large scale while striving to deliver the best product we can.

Our technology stack includes AWS (Lambdas, ECS, AWS Batch, RDS, DynamoDB, SFN, etc.), TypeScript, Python, Redis, and more.

My stack includes NodeJS, TypeScript, Python, AWS and Serverless architecture, and I'm always glad to expand my stack when needed. I truly enjoy solving architectural and coding problems, working with my colleagues, and growing together with them. Besides all that, I enjoy being involved in other areas besides the R&D.

As a part of the Security Engine team in Bridgecrew, we maintain different Open Source projects. A great example of a project we maintain is Checkov - a static code analysis tool for infrastructure-as-code, and vulnerabilities scanner for images and packages, with more than 6m downloads.

An example of a feature I led is the Supply Chain Graph (see link attached), starting from the first phase of its development process across different teams, design review, tasks breakdown, implementation, up to the delivery to production.

As Bio-T Medical's R&D group continues to grow, a new team has emerged - the DevOps team. When this opportunity arose, I jumped into this new challenge to learn new things and to contribute to the R&D group in new ways. As a DevOps engineer, my main mission is designing and implementing our transition from a CloudFormation IaaC solution to Terraform, while applying Cloud best practices to our infrastructure, so the developers would love to use them and our customers would enjoy uninterrupted service.

Bio-T is a Medical IoT cloud platform company, providing the optimal solution for device manufacturers who decide to provide cloud-based values to their users and resellers.

I am a software engineer and a full stack developer. My stack includes mostly AWS, Spring Boot, React and React Native, with emphasis on Backend & DevOps.

Using Spring Boot I build and maintain micro-services that in some cases use different AWS services (S3, SQS, Kinesis, IoT, etc). I maintain the infrastructure as a code using Cloudformation templates.

Using React Native I build applications for both iOS and Android. The applications I build usually have to be able to communicate with other specific devices through BLE, using different predefined protocols. As I build an application, I do integration with the customer who has built a device to make sure the application and the device can communicate. That how we get a Device-Application-Server cycle - a full and running IoT environment.

name: Shai Yallin
headline: SDLC Optimizer; Refactoring Artist; DevEx consultant; former engineering manager @ Wix; systems thinker. I will help you level-up your engineers, deal with your growing pains, and write software faster.

currentPosition.position: Freelance Software Engineer and DevEx consultant
currentPosition.description:
As startups grow, we make hard choices and often have to cut corners and accumulate technical debt. 
At some point, the interest rate on this technical debt becomes so massive that it could grind the R&D organization to a halt. Dev velocity is impacted, deadlines are missed - while the engineering team work endless hours and burn out. 

It doesn’t have to be like that.

As a founding member of the Wix Engineering organization, and as the principal engineer of the Orbs Blockchain platform, I gained a lot of experience with cutting edge engineering methodologies and culture. For early-stage startups, I can work with the CTO or VP R&D to build the right team and system, assist in making good compromises and help think of how current decisions might affect the ability to manage the engineering organization and process in the long run. For established startups that are stuck in mud, or feel their engineering organization could work much better, I can step in and help get you out of the mud, for instance by leading (hands-on) a gradual rewrite project with an engineer or two from your organization, yielding both a better system and engineers who can retain the methodologies and train others.

name: Moran Zabatani
headline: Fractional CTO / VP R&D | AI & Operational Optimization | Aligning Product, Technology & Business | Engineering Leadership Advisor

currentPosition.position: Fractional CTO / VP R&D | Advisor – Product, Technology & Operations
currentPosition.description:
After leading engineering and R&D organizations and working closely with CEOs in fast-moving, high-ambiguity environments, I now work as a fractional CTO / VP R&D and advisor.

I partner with founders, CEOs, and leadership teams at moments where early decisions carry long-term consequences, around product direction, technology strategy, operational efficiency, and how teams structure to execute effectively. I also support companies exploring how AI and automation can be applied meaningfully within their products and workflows.

My focus is helping teams make sound decisions early - on positioning, product scope, architecture, and operating models, and identifying opportunities where better processes, tools, or AI capabilities can improve how teams operate and deliver value.
I’m most effective in early-stage and transition phases, where focus, trade-offs and alignment matter, and where teams are moving from ideas and experimentation into practical execution.

Where I’m typically brought in:

Founder & Executive Advisory (Product, Strategy & AI Opportunities):
 Partner with founders and early executive teams building technology products during formation and validation stages, helping clarify product vision, positioning, and market focus, while identifying opportunities to leverage AI or automation across product and operational workflows.

Fractional CTO / VP R&D (Strategic Technical Leadership):
 Provide part-time technology leadership to align technical direction with business goals, offering architectural guidance, decision frameworks, and operating model design: clarifying roles, ownership, and decision-making to support scalable systems and reduce execution risk.

Leadership Growth, Coaching & Hiring Advisory:
 Support senior leaders as they grow into more complex roles, developing the judgment needed to lead through change and complexity, while partnering on critical hiring decisions through role definition, interview participation, and hiring calibration.

Joined Fintastic during a critical growth phase of a complex fintech SaaS product, leveraging advanced probability science, risk-derived planning, and multi-dimensional modeling. It uses mathematical optimizations to deliver fast, accurate, actionable insights.

Worked closely with the CEO and executive leadership on product direction, technical strategy, and organizational focus, balancing customer demands, scalability risks, and long-term platform health, with end-to-end responsibility for R&D, engineering, platform, security, and delivery execution in a highly competitive market.

Key decisions and business impact:

* Reframed product and technical priorities to support differentiation in a crowded market, helping improve customer retention and enable onboarding of new enterprise clients.

* Established enterprise-grade security and compliance foundations (SOC 2, ISO 27001), removing a key blocker in sales cycles and enabling access to larger customers.

* Introduced AI-assisted and quality-driven delivery practices, shortening feedback loops from weeks to hours and improving engineering throughput without increasing headcount.

* Stabilized execution during a fundraising period, contributing to momentum toward a successful Series A process shortly after my departure.

This work took place during a period of active preparation for a Series A funding round, where execution stability, product maturity, and technical credibility were critical. During this time, the company secured an enterprise customer that transitioned from a competing platform, reinforcing the strength of the product and platform direction. 

I stepped away following an international relocation.

currentPosition.description:
IAMOPS offers DevOps and Monitoring services for startups and high-growth companies in Israel and the United States.   

We work with startups across various industries, including fintech, telecom, gaming, health, ed-tech, security, and more.  

OUR SERVICES  

1: DevOps as a Service 

We source and train the best DevOps to take complete charge of your DevOps workload.  

We take responsibility for: 

Cloud Management: We ensure your cloud infrastructure is always efficient, optimized, and secure, following the principles of a well-architected framework. 

CI/CD & Automation: we provide a smooth flow between the production and operation environment, increasing automation to reduce manual work hours and errors. 

Cost Optimization: We minimize overall Cloud Costs to ensure you’re always within budget, and you can choose the best pricing plan for you. 

Security and Compliance: We make sure your infrastructure is always built to surpass strict penetration testing, and your product meets the criteria for ISO 27001 Compliance. 

2: NOC as a Service 

Our best-in-class 24/7 NOC team handles your monitoring environment's design, set-up, and implementation, so your product can be monitored 24/7.  

We take complete responsibility for: 

Monitoring System Design: Our NOC teams assemble best-in-market monitoring technologies to design a comprehensive monitoring solution. 

Monitoring System Set-up: We set up and implement best practices for your monitoring environment, configuring any required tools. 

24/7 Monitoring: We monitor your infrastructure 24/7 with three engineers in 8-hour shifts so that you can focus on growth. 

Incident Management: We provide immediate Tier 1 Support in case of any alerts, executing the relevant procedures. 

Risk Mitigation: We take complete charge of all your documentation, reporting any incidents immediately. 

CONTACT US:
